content:
# Allergen Dialogue Script Card

**Tool ID:** T007-01
**Block:** B007 (Allergen Customer Dialogue Script)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Front-of-House Staff
**Time Required:** 10-20 minutes to learn; ongoing reference
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

A ready-to-use script card that removes the guesswork from allergen conversations. A customer with a severe nut allergy does not want to hear "I think it is fine." They want confidence, clarity, and competence. This card gives your team the exact words to use in three common scenarios.

**Built for Front-of-House Staff working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Laminated A5 card with scripted responses for three allergen scenarios: proactive ask, reactive response, and severe allergy escalation. Positioned at each till point for instant reference during customer interactions.

This tool supports **Allergen Customer Dialogue Script** (B007) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** All Front-of-House Staff (baristas, till operators, floor staff)
**Time Required:** 10-20 minutes to read and practise; then instant reference during service
**Frequency:** Referenced during every customer allergen enquiry

**What You'll Need:**
- This script card laminated and positioned at each till/service point
- Allergen Matrix (T005-01) within reach at the counter
- Knowledge of where to find the Kitchen Lead or Manager for escalation
- Confidence -- which comes from practising these scripts before service

**Steps:**
1. Read through all three scenarios below
2. Practise saying the scripts out loud (yes, out loud -- it matters)
3. Role-play with a colleague during a quiet period
4. Keep this card visible at your till point
5. When a customer asks about allergens, use the appropriate script
6. NEVER guess. ALWAYS check. If unsure, escalate to the Kitchen Lead or Manager

---

## Template

### Scenario 1: Proactive Ask (You Ask the Customer)

**When to use:** At the point of order, especially for new customers or when serving food items.

**The Script:**

> "Hi! Before I take your order, do you have any allergies or dietary requirements I should know about?"

**If the customer says YES:**

> "No problem at all. Let me check our allergen information for you."
> *(Consult the Allergen Matrix T005-01)*
> "So the [item name] contains [list allergens in bold from the matrix]. Would you like me to suggest an alternative?"

**If the customer says NO:**

> "Great, thank you for letting me know."
> *(Continue with the order)*

**Key rules:**
- Ask EVERY customer when serving food, not just those who look like they might have allergies
- Do not assume. Do not profile. Ask everyone the same way
- If the customer seems rushed, still ask -- it takes 5 seconds

---

### Scenario 2: Reactive Response (Customer Asks You)

**When to use:** A customer asks "Does this have nuts in it?" or "Is this gluten-free?" or any allergen-related question.

**The Script:**

> "That is a really important question -- let me check our allergen information to make sure I give you accurate information."
> *(Consult the Allergen Matrix T005-01)*
> "I can confirm that the [item name] [contains / does not contain] [specific allergen]. It is also worth knowing that [any cross-contact risks, e.g., 'our kitchen handles nuts so there is a risk of cross-contact']."

**If you are not sure:**

> "I want to make sure I give you the right information. Let me check with my manager / Kitchen Lead -- I will be right back."
> *(Go to the Kitchen Lead or Manager. Do NOT guess.)*

**NEVER say any of the following:**
- "I think it is fine"
- "It should be OK"
- "I am pretty sure there is no [allergen] in it"
- "Nobody has ever had a problem with it"
- "It does not taste like it has nuts in it"

**ALWAYS say:**
- "Let me check for you"
- "I will confirm with our allergen information"
- "Let me ask the kitchen to be sure"

---

### Scenario 3: Severe Allergy Escalation

**When to use:** A customer says they have a severe allergy, mentions anaphylaxis, carries an EpiPen, or you feel the risk is higher than a standard enquiry.

**The Script:**

> "Thank you for telling me. I take this very seriously. Let me get my manager / Kitchen Lead so we can make sure we handle your order safely."
> *(Immediately find the Shift Manager or Kitchen Lead. Do not attempt to serve the customer yourself.)*

**Manager / Kitchen Lead takes over:**

> "I understand you have a severe [allergen] allergy. I want to walk you through exactly what is in the item you are interested in, and explain our kitchen setup so you can make an informed decision."
> *(Go through the full ingredient list, not just the allergen matrix)*
> "Our kitchen does handle [allergens present in the kitchen], so I want to be transparent that while the product itself does not contain [allergen], there is a cross-contact risk from our shared preparation environment."
> "Would you like to go ahead, or would you prefer me to suggest items with the lowest risk?"

**If you cannot guarantee safety:**

> "I want to be honest with you -- given the severity of your allergy, I am not confident that we can guarantee a completely [allergen]-free environment in our kitchen. I would rather be upfront about that than put you at risk. Can I suggest [safe alternative] or would you prefer to choose something else?"

**Key rules for severe allergy situations:**
- ALWAYS escalate to the most senior person on shift
- NEVER prepare the order yourself if you are unsure about cross-contact
- Use clean, dedicated utensils if proceeding (fresh chopping board, clean knife, clean gloves)
- If in ANY doubt, it is better to say "I cannot guarantee your safety" than to risk a reaction
- Document the interaction on the Allergen Conversation Log (T007-03)

---

### Quick Reference: What to Do

| Situation | Action | Script Starter |
|-----------|--------|---------------|
| Taking any food order | Ask proactively | "Do you have any allergies or dietary requirements?" |
| Customer asks about a specific allergen | Check the matrix | "Let me check our allergen information for you." |
| Customer mentions severe allergy or EpiPen | Escalate immediately | "Let me get my manager to assist you personally." |
| You are unsure about any ingredient | Never guess | "I want to make sure I give you accurate information -- let me check." |
| Customer asks about cross-contact | Be honest | "Our kitchen handles [allergens]. I want to be transparent about that." |
| A new or seasonal item with no allergen info | Do not serve until verified | "This is a new item and I need to confirm the allergen information before I can serve it." |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Front-of-House Staff (read and understood) | _____ | _____ | _____ |
| Manager (verified scripts with team) | _____ | _____ | _____ |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Staff Say "I Think It Is Fine" Instead of Checking

**Why it happens:** The queue is long, the customer is in a hurry, and the barista thinks they remember the allergen status from last time. They give a verbal assurance without checking the matrix. If they are wrong, a customer could have a serious allergic reaction.
**How to prevent:** Zero-tolerance policy on guessing. The Allergen Matrix is at the till for a reason -- it takes 10 seconds to check. Train staff that "let me check" is the ONLY acceptable response. A 10-second check is faster than an ambulance call.
**If it is already happening:** Role-play it at the next team meeting. Ask a team member: "Does the carrot cake have nuts?" If they answer from memory, that is your problem. If they reach for the matrix, that is your standard.

### Risk 2: Allergen Conversation Not Documented for High-Risk Situations

**Why it happens:** A customer with a severe peanut allergy orders a cake. The Shift Manager checks everything carefully, serves the order safely, but nobody writes it down. If the customer later reports a reaction, there is no evidence of the precautions taken.
**How to prevent:** For any severe allergy interaction (Scenario 3), log it on the Allergen Conversation Log (T007-03). It takes 2 minutes and provides crucial evidence of due diligence.
**If it is already happening:** Introduce the log this week. Keep it behind the counter. Brief the team: any time a customer mentions severe allergy, anaphylaxis, or EpiPen, the conversation gets logged.

### Risk 3: New Staff Not Trained on the Script Before Serving Customers

**Why it happens:** A new barista starts on a busy Monday. They are shown how to operate the espresso machine but nobody covers allergen dialogue. On their first solo shift, a customer asks about nuts and the barista freezes.
**How to prevent:** Allergen dialogue training is part of Day 1 induction, before the new starter serves any customer. Use the Role-Play Assessment (T007-04) to verify competence. No solo serving until they have passed.
**If it is already happening:** Run a 15-minute allergen briefing at the next team meeting. Cover all three scenarios. Make it clear this is not optional knowledge.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Adapt the language to match your brand voice. If your style is casual and friendly, the scripts should be too -- but the content must remain accurate. You might say "Hey, any allergies I should know about?" instead of the formal version.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand may have specific allergen dialogue scripts. Use this as a supplement or training tool. Ensure your scripts cover the same three scenarios.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Space is limited. Print Scenario 2 (Reactive Response) as a mini card taped to the inside of the serving hatch. This is the most commonly needed script.
**Bakery Cafe:** Your products are likely to contain more allergens (gluten, eggs, milk, nuts). Emphasise the proactive ask for every bakery item -- do not assume the customer knows what is in a croissant.

**Making it yours:**
- Adjust the wording to match your team culture and brand voice
- Add your specific menu items to the practice scenarios
- Include your Kitchen Lead and Manager names on the escalation card
- Practise the scripts quarterly as a team -- confidence comes from repetition

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T007-02 -- Allergen Communication SOP (L2, SOP)
- T007-03 -- Allergen Conversation Log (L2, tracker)
- T007-04 -- Allergen Dialogue Role-Play Assessment (L1, assessment)
- T007-05 -- Quarterly Allergen Mystery Shopper Scorecard (L3, scorecard)

**Related blocks:**
- B005

**Parent block:** B007 -- Allergen Customer Dialogue Script
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
